datasets_used: ACDC (200 cardiac MRI images from 100 patients)
baselines_compared: MT, UA-MT, EM, DCT, CCT, CPS, ICT, DAN, URPC, SSNet, ICT-Med, FixMatch, SCPNet, BCP-Net, CTCT, S4CVnet, MedFCT, CnT-B, DiffRect, CPC-SAM
hyperparameters: UNet backbone, 224×224 input size, batch size 16, SGD optimizer (weight decay 1e-4, momentum 0.9), 300 epochs, learning rate 0.01, channel dropout 50%, r=1000, τ=0.95, channel dimension D=128
headline_result: SemSim achieved 0.896 DSC and 2.3mm 95HD on ACDC with 10% labeled data
ablations: Table IV: All three consistency constraints (intra, cross, unsupervised) needed for optimal performance (0.872 DSC vs 0.844 without cross-image)
Table V: Optimal sub-regions N=4 (0.896 DSC) vs N=1 (0.888 DSC) on ACDC
Spatial-aware fusion module (SFM) contributes 0.5% DSC improvement (0.861 vs 0.872)
improvement_over_baseline: SemSim outperforms CPC-SAM by +0.6% DSC and -0.2mm 95HD on ACDC (10% labeled), +0.8% DSC and -5.6mm 95HD on ISIC (10% labeled), and +5.8% DSC on PROMISE12 (7% labeled)
best_result: SemSim achieved 0.896 DSC and 2.3mm 95HD on ACDC with 10% labeled data
title: SemSim: Revisiting Weak-to-Strong Consistency from a Semantic Similarity Perspective for Semi-supervised Medical Image Segmentation
